label: Differential equations

node.label: Differential equations
node.definition: Ordinary, partial, and stochastic differential equations describe continuous change and physical dynamics.
node.why_it_matters: In this atlas graph, Differential equations is modeled as depending on Calculus & automatic differentiation theory, Numerical analysis and feeding downstream nodes such as Dynamical systems & control theory, Neuro-symbolic & physics-informed learning, Flow matching & transport objectives.
examples: atlas downstream link to Dynamical systems & control theory
atlas downstream link to Neuro-symbolic & physics-informed learning
atlas downstream link to Flow matching & transport objectives
limitations: Its abstractions require explicit assumptions; a mathematically valid result can still be irrelevant to a mismatched empirical setting. Its direct dependencies include Calculus & automatic differentiation theory, Numerical analysis.

dependencies.rationale: Differential equations rests on the formal or computational capabilities supplied by Calculus & automatic differentiation theory.
Differential equations rests on the formal or computational capabilities supplied by Numerical analysis.
